mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-cut periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below 9.5 percent, it signals a labor market inflection point that boosts consumer confidence and aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 9.5, or the signal loses predictive power after 2012.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls confirms a broad-based economic expansion, reducing equity risk premiums and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative directional accuracy below 50% over a 5-year out-of-sample test, or forward returns do not exceed the unconditional mean by at least 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained industrial production growth above trend indicates robust corporate capacity utilization and margin expansion, which directly translates into positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window, or INDPRO growth decouples from SPX returns during peak capacity utilization periods.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent on a quarterly basis confirms economic momentum that supports corporate revenue forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if 20-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5%, or if the signal reverses direction during recessionary quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a rising federal funds rate compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a positive coefficient in a multivariate regression, or forward returns remain positive despite simultaneous CPI and rate increases exceeding historical thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: Continuous month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects sustained business hiring, which reduces equity risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward directional accuracy falls below 50% across all market cycles, or the signal produces negative forward returns in more than 60% of positive payroll growth months.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales exceeding 1.5 percent signals a demand shock that erodes corporate earnings expectations, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict returns during periods of strong fiscal stimulus.
